$comment: Curated picoCTF challenge manifest for pwnkit benchmarking. Each entry must have a unique id, category, and either a live URL (web challenges) or file download URLs (offline challenges). Challenges are drawn from picoGym and recent competitions.

challenges.name: Obedient Cat
challenges.description: This file has a flag in plain sight (ooh those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those those).

challenges.name: Mod 26
challenges.description: Cryptography can be easy, do you know what ROT13 is? cvpbPGS{arkg_gvzr_V'yy_gel_2_ebhaqf_bs_ebg13_jdJBFOXk}

challenges.name: Wave a flag
challenges.description: Can you invoke help flags from a program from the command line?

challenges.name: Nice netcat...
challenges.description: There is a nice program that you can talk to by using this command in a shell: nc mercury.picoctf.net 35652
Cryptography can be easy, do you know what ROT13 is?

challenges.name: caesar
challenges.description: Decrypt this message.

challenges.name: dont-use-client-side
challenges.description: Can you break into this super secure portal?

challenges.name: logon
challenges.description: The factory is hiding things from all of its users. Can you login as the admin?

challenges.name: where are the robots
challenges.description: Can you find the robots? https://jupiter.challenges.picoctf.org/problem/56830/

challenges.name: client-side-again
challenges.description: Can you break into this super secure portal? I bet you can't!

challenges.name: picobrowser
challenges.description: This website can be rendered only by picobrowser, go and determine what is special about it.

challenges.name: Irish-Name-Repo 1
challenges.description: There is a website running at the given URL. Do you think you can log in?

challenges.name: Irish-Name-Repo 2
challenges.description: There is a website running at the given URL. Someone has bypassed the login before, can you do it too?

challenges.name: Irish-Name-Repo 3
challenges.description: There is a secure website running at the given URL. Try to see if you can login!

challenges.name: Inspect HTML
challenges.description: Can you get the flag just by inspecting the website?

challenges.name: login
challenges.description: My dog-sitter's website has a login page. See if you can find the password.

challenges.name: caas
challenges.description: Now presenting cowsay as a service.

challenges.name: SQL Direct
challenges.description: Connect to this PostgreSQL server and find the flag!

challenges.name: SQLiLite
challenges.description: Can you login to this website?

challenges.name: bases
challenges.description: What does this bXkgc3VwZXIgc2VjcmV0IHZhbHVl mean?

challenges.name: First Grep
challenges.description: Can you find the flag in file?

challenges.name: strings it
challenges.description: Can you find the flag in file without running it?

challenges.name: What Lies Within
challenges.description: There is something in the building. Can you retrieve the flag?

challenges.name: information
challenges.description: Files can always be changed in a secret way. Can you find the flag?

challenges.name: Matryoshka doll
challenges.description: Matryoshka dolls are a set of wooden dolls of decreasing size placed one inside another. What's the flag?

challenges.name: tunn3l v1s10n
challenges.description: We found this file. Recover the flag.

challenges.name: Glory of the Garden
challenges.description: This garden contains more than what meets the eye.

challenges.name: So Meta
challenges.description: Find the flag in this picture.

challenges.name: Easy Peasy
challenges.description: A one-time pad is unbreakable, but can you manage to recover the flag? nc mercury.picoctf.net 36981

challenges.name: New Caesar
challenges.description: We found a brand new type of encryption. Can you break the code?

challenges.name: basic-mod1
challenges.description: We found this weird message being passed around on the servers. Take each number mod 37 and map it to a character.

challenges.name: basic-mod2
challenges.description: A new modular challenge! Take each number, mod 41, and find the modular inverse.

challenges.name: rail-fence
challenges.description: A type of transposition cipher is the rail fence cipher.

challenges.name: substitution0
challenges.description: A message has been encrypted using a simple substitution cipher.

challenges.name: substitution1
challenges.description: A second message has been encrypted using a substitution cipher.

challenges.name: substitution2
challenges.description: It seems that another encrypted message has been intercepted.

challenges.name: transposition-trial
challenges.description: Our data got corrupted on the way here. Luckily, nothing was lost.

challenges.name: Vigenere
challenges.description: Can you decrypt this message?

challenges.name: file-types
challenges.description: This file was found among some files marked confidential but it doesn't have an extension.

challenges.name: hideme
challenges.description: Every file gets a flag. The more the merrier.

challenges.name: patchme.py
challenges.description: Can you get the flag? Run this Python program in the same directory as this encrypted flag.

challenges.name: unpackme.py
challenges.description: Can you get the flag? Reverse engineer this Python program.

challenges.name: Safe Opener
challenges.description: Can you open this safe? I forgot the key to the safe.

challenges.name: GDB baby step 1
challenges.description: Can you figure out what is in the eax register at the end of the main function?

challenges.name: GDB baby step 2
challenges.description: Can you figure out what is in the eax register at the end of the main function?

challenges.name: findme
challenges.description: Help me find the flag for this website.

challenges.name: More SQLi
challenges.description: Can you find the flag on this website?

challenges.name: Java Code Analysis!?!
challenges.description: BookShelf Pico, my Java application, is insecure. Can you hack it?

challenges.name: buffer overflow 0
challenges.description: Smash the stack. Let's start off simple, can you overflow the correct buffer?

challenges.name: buffer overflow 1
challenges.description: Control the return address. Can you redirect execution to the win function?
